page: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 2000 GB HDD. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the virtual machine runs on the Xen hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that the h1.2xlarge exhibits modest compute and memory performance. In stress-ng and Geekbench single-core evaluations, the instance scores in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance falls in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests, including both small-block cached and large-block uncached reads and writes, consistently rank in the bottom 25%. However, Passmark CPU and memory scores reach average levels, and the CPU physics test also registers in the middle 50% tier.

This instance is designed for workloads requiring cost-effective local HDD storage rather than high-performance compute or memory throughput. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all rank in the bottom 25% tier, indicating that high-throughput transactional databases are not ideal. Instead, the server is suited for sequential data access, data warehousing, log processing, or large-scale cold storage applications where storage capacity per unit of compute is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It includes a bundled 2000 GB local HDD and operates on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show modest performance, with single-core CPU and text processing scores in the bottom 10%, and multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the bottom 25%. However, Passmark CPU and memory scores achieve average ratings. Given its hardware profile, the h1.2xlarge is best suited for workloads that require high-capacity, cost-effective local HDD storage rather than intensive computational power, such as log processing, data warehousing, and sequential-access storage applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2000 GB local HDD, designed for cost-effective sequential data access and log processing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services h1.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2000 GB local HDD for cost-effective data workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon processor and high-capacity local HDD storage for cost-effective sequential data workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
Bundled 2000 GB local HDD storage for high-capacity data needs
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Xen hypervisor
Average performance in Passmark CPU and memory benchmarks
Best suited for log processing, data warehousing, and cold storage
